pull.title: [Cute,Sm100] Introduce a flexible lambda-based R2P masking
pull.body:
Introduce a flexible lambda-based R2P masking. Also switching from 24-col to 32-col bitmasks with inline PTX shifts.

### Motivation

The masking code has 3 separate implementations (`mask_r2p`, `mask_r2p_transposed`, `mask_r2p_dual_bound`), each reimplementing the same 24-col R2P loop with slight variations, making the code harder to maintain.

The current R2P masking also uses **24-column chunks** to avoid a shift-by-32 undefined behavior problem. Shifting 32-bit integer by 32 is [UB in LLVM IR](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#shl-instruction), and the compiler exploits this to eliminate boundary bit tests. Staying at 24 (< 32) sidesteps the issue, but 24 is an awkward non-power-of-2 choice: 
- Need more chunks to cover typical power-of-2 sequences/tile sizes
- Computing bitmasks for 24-cols in general is less intuitive than 32-cols.

### What this PR does

**1. Unified `mask_r2p_lambda` with 32-col chunks.** A single generic R2P masking function that takes a lambda `mask_gen_fn(chunk_idx) -> Uint32` returning the 32-bit bitmask for each chunk. All masking variants (causal, local/window, transposed for backward) now compose `r2p_bitmask_below` and `r2p_bitmask_above` primitives via this lambda interface. This replaces the three separate functions with one. (Enabling R2P path in local bwd path is a by-product, but sadly not seeing much perf improvement).

**2. Inline PTX shifts to avoid shift-by-32 UB.** We use inline PTX `shl.b32` / `shr.u32` to compute the 32-bit bitmasks. [PTX shift](https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/parallel-thread-execution/#logic-and-shift-instructions-shl) semantics clamp shift amounts ≥ the register width to the width itself (producing 0), so shifting by exactly 32 is well-defined. Because the inline assembly bypasses LLVM IR entirely, the optimizer cannot exploit UB or insert extended-precision instructions. Refer to [this gist](https://gist.github.com/Alkaid-Benetnash/96999fe200d6fc9ba1428a00612eee77) if you are interested in more details on this "LLVM IR UB" matter.

**3. Minor:**
  - `causal_row_offset` semantic cleanup.** The `+1` that made `causal_row_offset` carry "exclusive right bound" semantics is moved to the usage site (`col_limit_right = row_idx + causal_row_offset + 1`), making the offset value itself more intuitive.
  - Prior inline ptx for `shr_u32` used `shr.s32`. Seems to be a typo.

**4. sm100-only:** Currently only focusing on sm100. sm90 code paths are left untouched.

### Passing all tests

Tested with (with #2312 patched)
```
export CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7
export FLASH_ATTENTION_CUTE_DSL_CACHE_ENABLED=1
# test_flash_attn.py excluding the 'test_flash_attn_kvcache' known to hang (https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention/issues/2278)
FLASH_ATTENTION_FAKE_TENSOR=1 pytest -n 256 -x tests/cute/test_flash_attn.py
FLASH_ATTENTION_FAKE_TENSOR=0 pytest -n 8 -x tests/cute/test_flash_attn.py -k 'not test_flash_attn_kvcache'
# 27548 passed, 15552 skipped, 1218 warnings in 148.62s (0:02:28)
# All other tests
pytest -n 8 -x tests/cute/ --ignore=tests/cute/test_flash_attn.py
# 22030 passed, 18971 skipped, 78 xfailed, 143103 warnings in 1217.42s (0:20:17)
```

### SASS-level impact (thanks Claude)

Compiled SASS comparison (SM100, seqlen=1024, d=128, bf16):

| Kernel | Before (24-col) | After (32-col) | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| fwd_causal | 6186 lines | 6178 lines | **-8** |
| fwd_local | 6460 lines | 6444 lines | **-16** |
| bwd | 6242 lines | 6234 lines | **-8** |
| **Total** | | | **-32** |

Example SASS instruction deltas breakdown :

**fwd_causal (-8):**

| Instruction | Before | After | Δ | Category |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SHF.L.U32 | 42 | 30 | -12 | Masking: fewer left-shifts (fewer chunks) |
| SHF.R.U32.HI | 38 | 46 | +8 | Masking: new right-shifts (32-col) |
| LOP3.LUT | 110 | 98 | -12 | Masking: fewer scalar bit tests |
| ISETP.NE.U32.AND | 14 | 10 | -4 | Masking: fewer col-0 tests |
| ISETP.GT.AND | 16 | 24 | +8 | Masking: new col-31 tests |
| VIADDMNMX | 11 | 10 | -1 | Masking: fewer chunk boundary clamps |
| VIMNMX.RELU | 1 | 0 | -1 | Masking: first-chunk clamp removed |
| VIMNMX | 2 | 0 | -2 | Masking: first-chunk clamp removed |
| | | | **-16** | **Masking subtotal** |
| IMAD.U32 | 22 | 24 | +2 | Non-masking (regalloc noise) |
| IMAD.MOV | 0 | 1 | +1 | " |
| MOV | 97 | 99 | +2 | " |
| NOP | 8 | 12 | +4 | " |
| IADD3 | 9 | 11 | +2 | " |
| VIADD | 7 | 5 | -2 | " |
| ULEA | 56 | 55 | -1 | " |
| | | | **+8** | **Non-masking subtotal** |
| | | | **-8 ✓** | **Total** |

SASS breakdown for `fwd_local` and `bwd` are omitted --> basically following the same pattern.

The dominant masking cost (SEL instructions, conditional changing registers based on bitmask) is identical across both versions.
So this change is not expected to impact performance much.

### Performance benchmarks

#### Benchmark Used

- flash-attention's own `python benchmarks/benchmark_attn.py`
    - Modified to sweep local/windowed shapes
    - Set repeat=1000 to get stable perf numbers
- [scripts from flex-attention](https://github.com/meta-pytorch/attention-gym/blob/main/benchmarks/flex_perf.py) `python benchmarks/flex_perf.py --config benchmarks/configs/flash_b200.yaml`
    - Updated triton `do_bench` param to be `warmup=1000, rep=500` for stable perf numbers

#### Summary of results

Based on the SASS analysis, the performance is expected to remain unchanged or even slightly-better.

Measured numbers mostly agree with that, but there are indeed outliers, all from `flex_perf`.
Those differences are likely from run-to-run variance (I can't explain such gaps otherwise).

**Notation for speedup:** Δ = `(time_before − time_after) / time_before × 100%`. Positive --> faster.

Outlier runs with |Δ| > 5%:

- B200 flex_perf, causal M=4096 D=128 Hkv=4: Bwd -7.5%
- B200 flex_perf, causal M=8192 D=128 Hkv=4: Bwd -5.6%
- B200 flex_perf, noop M=8192 D=64 Hkv=4: Fwd -8.8%, Bwd +5.1%
- B200 flex_perf, noop M=8192 D=128 Hkv=4: Fwd -14.2%
- B200 flex_perf, noop M=8192 D=128 Hkv=16: Bwd +6.0%
- GB200 flex_perf, causal M=16384 D=128 Hkv=4: Bwd +10.9%
- GB200 flex_perf, causal M=32768 D=128 Hkv=4: Fwd +8.9%
- GB200 flex_perf, causal M=16384 D=128 Hkv=16: Bwd +7.6%
- GB200 flex_perf, document_mask M=2048 D=64 Hkv=16: Fwd +13.7%
- GB200 flex_perf, document_mask M=2048 D=128 Hkv=16: Fwd +10.0%
- GB200 flex_perf, noop M=32768 D=128 Hkv=4: Fwd +7.8%

Also attaching the detailed perf numbers here:

#### B200 — benchmark_attn

SM100, headdim=128, seqlen=8192, batch=4, nheads=16, bf16.

| causal | 1.146ms / 960 TF | 1.150ms / 956 TF | -0.3% | 3.275ms / 839 TF | 3.296ms / 834 TF | -0.6% |
| symmetric(512) | 0.391ms / 681 TF | 0.390ms / 683 TF | +0.3% | 6.022ms / 111 TF | 5.981ms / 111 TF | +0.7% |
| symmetric(1024) | 0.630ms / 819 TF | 0.631ms / 818 TF | -0.2% | 6.054ms / 213 TF | 6.038ms / 214 TF | +0.3% |
| symmetric(2048) | 1.049ms / 917 TF | 1.044ms / 922 TF | +0.5% | 6.046ms / 398 TF | 6.057ms / 397 TF | -0.2% |
| symmetric(4096) | 1.673ms / 986 TF | 1.674ms / 985 TF | -0.1% | 6.046ms / 682 TF | 6.042ms / 682 TF | +0.1% |
| left(512) | 0.274ms / 487 TF | 0.273ms / 489 TF | +0.4% | 6.072ms / 55 TF | 6.054ms / 55 TF | +0.3% |
| left(1024) | 0.388ms / 664 TF | 0.389ms / 664 TF | -0.3% | 6.065ms / 106 TF | 6.064ms / 106 TF | 0.0% |
| left(2048) | 0.600ms / 802 TF | 0.597ms / 806 TF | +0.5% | 6.072ms / 198 TF | 6.053ms / 199 TF | +0.3% |
| left(4096) | 0.921ms / 895 TF | 0.916ms / 901 TF | +0.5% | 6.074ms / 340 TF | 6.039ms / 341 TF | +0.6% |
